description: Two card-endpoints. Both equal nodes. Pokemon-side labor-extraction register, the strongest in-corpus pair. Pikachu BUS-40's flavor text is the franchise's most explicit workforce-as-energy-resource citation: 'A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.' Many creatures, one power plant. Galar Mine RBC-160 is the corpus's only depicted Pokemon-side industrial-extraction site — underground tunnel, wooden support beams, mine-cart rails, dim ambient lighting, no figures. Together they anchor the two endpoints of the labor-extraction read on the Pokemon side: workforce-aggregation in flavor on one side, extraction-site infrastructure in art on the other. The connection has narrative weight that survives tag-collapse and synonym-merge — it isn't just 'both tagged labor.' Mirror required. Professional sigh.

other_candidates_considered: Labor hub member_cards population (E3 candidate a) — moot; labor hub doesn't have a member_cards field, population happens implicitly via tags_hub on cards. The implicit edge already exists. Surfaced as confirmation rather than action.

Galar Mine RBC-160 → Pikachu / Mudbray / Capacious Bucket 1:N branch (E3 candidate b) — rejected; Galar Mine is a location/Stadium card, not an institutional-anchor node. Forcing a 1:N from a card to other cards is the wrong shape; institutional fanouts come from character/hub nodes. If a future Nodeley pass commissions a galar-region-labor-infrastructure character node (parallel to galar-gym-challenge institution + galarian-regional-forms biome), the branch becomes natural.

Mudbray VIV-096 ↔ Pikachu BUS-40 1:1 — considered; both are creature-side labor entries, both flavor-text-cite their species' working role (Mudbray: 50x body weight load capacity; Pikachu: power plant aggregation). Rejected as weaker than the Galar Mine pairing because Mudbray's register is working-animal-as-companion (draft-animal/farm) while Pikachu's flavor specifically references the *industrial-scale aggregation* register that Galar Mine echoes. The Galar Mine pairing is tighter on the extraction-as-system axis.

Capacious Bucket RBC-156 was a labor-cluster candidate but its register is utility-tool-for-Water-Energy-search, not extraction; better filed under labor-as-chore (broader hub tag) than extracted into a 1:1 edge. Surface as future Nodeley candidate alongside Mudbray if a Pokemon-side labor sub-cluster commissions a character node.

Future-pass Discrete Lair candidate (E3 candidate d, modified): the labor cluster now has Pikachu BUS-40 + Galar Mine RBC-160 (mirrored) + Mudbray VIV-096 + Capacious Bucket RBC-156 + Grimer DAA-100 as a 5-card Pokemon-side labor stack. Once 2-3 more labor-tagged Pokemon cards land (Galarian Slowpoke's Galarica-seed agricultural register, a Pokemon working-class trainer card like Worker or Backpacker), a Pokemon-side labor Discrete Lair becomes commissionable. This pass's Pikachu ↔ Galar Mine mirror is the cohesion-anchor edge that lair would lean on; flagged for bbl-bundler.

Mr. Nodeley transformation toward a 'pokemon-labor-extraction' hub/character node — rejected this pass; current cluster is 5 cards and the [[labor]] hub already does the work. If the cluster grows past 8-10 cards or develops a clear sub-cluster axis (industrial-extraction vs working-animal vs chore-utility), a sub-hub becomes warrantable. Premature now.
